title: Day 4 (2/2) — Tokens, lab & API costs
summary: Part 2 of 2 · Steps 1–13 · §4–§7 + `day4.ipynb`. Start with **Day 4 (1/2)** if you haven't.

sections.heading: How tokenization evolved
sections.remember: Modern LLMs use **subword tokens** — between **character** vocab (tiny, hard) and **word** vocab (huge, OOV).
points: **Character-level** — ~100 symbols; network must learn spelling **and** meaning together.
**Word-level** — vocab explodes; rare words dropped.
**Subword / BPE** — fragments of words; fixed vocab; can represent rare terms via pieces.

sections.heading: Why subword tokens work
sections.remember: Tokens are an **engineering compromise** — fast, bounded vocab, match **stems + suffixes** in English.
points: Could use chars or giant dicts; **subwords won** on speed and learning.
**Stem pattern**: `play` + `ing` / `ed` — network reuses roots.
Rare names split into fragments instead of `<UNK>`.

sections.heading: Token ID vs embedding vector
sections.remember: First input to the model = **token ID** (integer). **Embedding vectors** are created **inside** the network — not the same thing.
points: text → **tokenizer** → IDs → model maps to **embeddings** internally.
Generation: predict next **token ID**, then decode to text.
Vectors covered in later weeks.

sections.heading: Tokenizer UI — common English
sections.remember: On **platform.openai.com/tokenizer**, common words often map **one word → one token**; tokenizer varies slightly by model.
points: Colored **space + word** = token encodes **start of new word** (leading space is part of the token).
Pick the model in the dropdown when investigating — small differences, usually not critical.

sections.heading: Word-start vs word-piece tokens
sections.remember: **` important`** (with leading space) ≠ **`unimportant`** pieces — word-start vs middle/end fragments.
points: **`crafted`** without leading space = suffix token reused in `handcrafted`, `witchcraft`.
Helps training; not a strict linguistic rule, just efficient BPE.

sections.heading: Rare words & token ratios
sections.remember: Rare text → **more tokens**; rule of thumb **~4 characters/token**, **~750 words per 1,000 tokens**; **code** costs more tokens.
points: **Pi**: often 3-digit chunks (~1000 dedicated tokens in GPT vocab).
**Shakespeare** ~900k words ≈ **~1.2M tokens** → why pricing is **per million tokens**.
Measure with tokenizer UI or **tiktoken** — don't guess for production.

sections.heading: Before the lab
sections.remember: Theory above · practice in **Steps 8–9** (`week1/day4.ipynb`) · memory concept in **Part 1 Step 4**.
points: UI: **platform.openai.com/tokenizer**
Code: **`tiktoken`** then **OpenAI API** memory demo

sections.heading: §6 Lab — Part 1: `tiktoken` (cells 1–4)
sections.remember: **`encode` / `decode` = local lookup** — no API call or API key for this section.
points: Kernel **`.venv`** · `encoding_for_model("gpt-4.1-mini")` (**chat** model).
Loop: print each **token ID** and `decode([id])` — **banoffee** → `ban` + `offee`.
sections.code:
import tiktoken
encoding = tiktoken.encoding_for_model("gpt-4.1-mini")
tokens = encoding.encode("Hi my name is Ed and I like banoffee pie")
for token_id in tokens:
    print(f"{token_id} = {encoding.decode([token_id])}")

sections.heading: §6 Lab — Part 2: memory illusion (cells 5–18)
sections.remember: Proves **Part 1 Step 4**: full **`messages`** (with **assistant** turn) required for follow-up questions.
points: Call 2 without history → doesn't know your name.
Call 3 with full thread → *Your name is Ed*.
**Checklist:** stateless · full conversation each time · illusion of memory · next-token prediction · more tokens = higher bill.

sections.heading: Context window
sections.remember: **Context window** = max **tokens** the model can consider at once (input + generated output so far) — includes **full conversation**, not just your last message.
points: Exceed the limit → request **fails** (“context too long”).
During generation, each new token is appended; the **entire sequence** is re-fed until completion — all of it must fit.
Uses: long **RAG** dumps, **few-shot** examples, **Claude Code** filling the window.
**Shakespeare** (~1.2M tokens) needs ~1M window — mainly **Gemini** class models today.

visual.caption: Example context limits (check providers for current values)

sections.heading: API costs — how billing works
sections.remember: **ChatGPT subscription ≠ API billing** — API is **pay-per-use** (input + output tokens); builds **your own product** on frontier models.
points: Prices fund **inference compute** (trillions of ops per call) + amortized training cost.
Charged per **input tokens** + **output tokens** (listed as **$/million tokens**).
**Catch 1:** input = **entire** `messages` thread + RAG + few-shot — grows every turn (you want that compute).
**Catch 2:** **reasoning / thinking tokens** count as **output** even when hidden (e.g. some **o-series** / internal traces).

sections.heading: Example prices & scale
sections.remember: **GPT-5** (example from **Vellum** leaderboard): ~$1.25 / 1M **input**, ~$10 / 1M **output** — a full Shakespeare-sized **output** ≈ $10; tiny prompts cost fractions of a cent.
points: Compare models: [vellum.ai/llm-leaderboard](https://www.vellum.ai/llm-leaderboard) — context window + API cost columns (Week 4 goes deeper).
**GPT-5 nano**: ~$0.05 / 1M input, ~$0.40 / 1M output — cheap experiments.
At **product scale**: model unit economics per user; for **learning**: single calls are usually very cheap.
**Prompt caching**: repeat identical prefix within minutes → cheaper input (OpenAI automatic; Claude varies).

sections.heading: Day 4 wrap-up
sections.remember: You now have foundations: **transformers**, **tokens**, **context window**, **stateless API**, **costs**, **agentic** preview.
points: Can call **OpenAI** and **Llama/Ollama**, summarize pages, compare frontier models.
Understand **memory illusion** and why **context engineering** matters.
**Week 1 ahead:** **Chat Completions** fluency, one-shot prompting, **streaming**, markdown/JSON, quick **business** build (Day 5).

cheatSheet: Subword tokens · ID ≠ vector · tiktoken lab
Context window = all input + output in flight
API $/M input + output · reasoning tokens bill too
Vellum leaderboard · nano models for cheap tests
